title: entandrophragma excelsum
intro: Entandrophragma excelsum, also known as the yew-like tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a high concentration of tannins and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Entandrophragma excelsum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as prevent the spread of infections. The bark of the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be administered orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
